# Engine-Specific Project Setup: Nookstead
*Generated by Game Studio Sub-Agents v1.0*

## Project Engine Configuration
**Project**: Nookstead
**Engine**: Custom Web Stack -- Phaser.js 3 (2D renderer) + Next.js 16 (web shell) + Colyseus (multiplayer server)
**Target Platforms**: Web (Desktop browsers, Mobile browsers)
**Setup Date**: 2026-02-13

---

## Architecture Overview

Nookstead uses a three-layer architecture rather than a single game engine:

```
Layer 1: CLIENT (Next.js 16 + Phaser.js 3)
  - Next.js serves the web shell, handles routing, auth, and non-game UI
  - Phaser.js 3 renders the game canvas (tile maps, sprites, animations)
  - Communicates with server via Colyseus.js WebSocket client

Layer 2: GAME SERVER (Node.js + Colyseus 0.15+)
  - Authoritative game state (positions, NPC schedules, game clock)
  - Manages rooms (one room = one server/world)
  - 10 ticks/sec for position synchronization
  - Communicates with AI Service via internal REST API

Layer 3: AI SERVICE (Node.js microservice)
  - Handles all LLM calls (Claude API)
  - NPC dialogue, planning, reflection, reactions
  - Memory stream management and retrieval
  - Queue-based request processing (BullMQ / Redis)
```

## Nx Monorepo Structure

All projects live within the existing Nx monorepo:

```
apps/
  game/                  # Next.js 16 app (EXISTS - scaffolded)
    src/
      app/               # App Router pages
        page.tsx          # Landing/lobby page
        play/
          page.tsx        # Game page (hosts Phaser canvas)
      components/

        ui/
          DialogueWindow.tsx
          HUD.tsx
          Inventory.tsx
      lib/

          scenes/
            BootScene.ts   # Asset loading
            TownScene.ts   # Main town map

          entities/
            Player.ts      # Player sprite + controls
            NPC.ts         # NPC sprite + behavior display
          systems/
            TilemapLoader.ts
            CameraController.ts
            InputManager.ts
        colyseus/
          client.ts        # Colyseus client connection
          room-handlers.ts # Room state change handlers
          state-sync.ts    # Client-side interpolation

  game-e2e/              # Playwright E2E tests (EXISTS)

  server/                # Colyseus game server (TO CREATE)
    src/
      rooms/
        TownRoom.ts       # Main game room
        schemas/
          PlayerState.ts
          NPCState.ts
          WorldState.ts
      entities/
        GameClock.ts      # 1 game hour = 1 real minute
        NPCScheduler.ts   # Executes NPC daily plans
      services/
        AIServiceClient.ts # REST client for AI service
      index.ts            # Server entry point

  ai-service/            # NPC AI microservice (TO CREATE)
    src/
      routes/
        dialogue.ts       # POST /agent/dialogue
        plan.ts           # POST /agent/plan
        reflect.ts        # POST /agent/reflect
        react.ts          # POST /agent/react
        conversation.ts   # POST /agent/conversation
      services/
        claude-client.ts  # Anthropic Claude API wrapper
        memory-retrieval.ts
        prompt-builder.ts
      models/
        agent.ts          # NPC agent data model
        memory.ts         # Memory stream model
      index.ts

libs/
  shared/                # Shared TypeScript types (TO CREATE)
    src/
      types/
        player.ts
        npc.ts
        world.ts
        messages.ts       # Client-server message types
      constants/
        game-config.ts    # Shared game constants (tick rate, time scale, etc.)
```

## Client Setup (Next.js + Phaser.js 3)

### Phaser.js Integration with Next.js

Phaser.js must be dynamically imported to avoid SSR issues (Phaser requires `window` and `document`).

```typescript
// apps/game/src/components/game/PhaserGame.tsx
'use client';

import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react';

export default function PhaserGame() {
  const gameRef = useRef<Phaser.Game | null>(null);
  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    // Dynamic import to avoid SSR
    import('phaser').then((Phaser) => {
      if (gameRef.current || !containerRef.current) return;

      const config: Phaser.Types.Core.GameConfig = {
        type: Phaser.AUTO, // WebGL with Canvas fallback
        parent: containerRef.current,
        width: 480,   // Base resolution
        height: 270,  // 16:9 at pixel-art scale
        pixelArt: true,
        roundPixels: true,
        scale: {
          mode: Phaser.Scale.FIT,
          autoCenter: Phaser.Scale.CENTER_BOTH,
        },
        physics: {
          default: 'arcade',
          arcade: { debug: false },
        },
        scene: [], // Loaded dynamically
      };

      gameRef.current = new Phaser.Game(config);
    });

    return () => {
      gameRef.current?.destroy(true);
      gameRef.current = null;
    };
  }, []);

  return <div ref={containerRef} style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }} />;
}
```

### Rendering Configuration
- **Base Resolution**: 480x270 pixels (scaled 3x to 1440x810 or 4x to 1920x1080)
- **Tile Size**: 16x16 pixels
- **Renderer**: WebGL with Canvas fallback (`Phaser.AUTO`)
- **Pixel Art Mode**: `pixelArt: true, roundPixels: true` for crisp pixel rendering
- **Scale Mode**: `Phaser.Scale.FIT` to fill available space while maintaining aspect ratio

### Tilemap Configuration
- **Editor**: Tiled (https://www.mapeditor.org/)
- **Export Format**: JSON (Tiled JSON Map Format)
- **Tile Layers** (bottom to top):
  1. `ground` -- terrain, water, paths
  2. `shadows` -- building shadows
  3. `objects_lower` -- walls, furniture base
  4. `characters` -- player and NPC sprites (render layer, not tilemap)
  5. `objects_upper` -- tree canopies, rooftops (rendered above characters)
  6. `collision` -- invisible collision layer for pathfinding
- **Asset Pack**: LimeZu Modern series (16x16 tilesets)

### Input System
```typescript
// Desktop: WASD/Arrow keys for movement, E for interact, I for inventory
// Mobile: Virtual joystick (left thumb), tap for interact

interface InputConfig {
  desktop: {
    move_up: ['W', 'UP'];
    move_down: ['S', 'DOWN'];
    move_left: ['A', 'LEFT'];
    move_right: ['D', 'RIGHT'];
    interact: ['E', 'SPACE'];
    inventory: ['I'];
    menu: ['ESC'];
  };
  mobile: {
    joystick: true;  // Virtual joystick for movement
    tap_interact: true;  // Tap NPC/object to interact
    swipe_menu: true;  // Swipe up for inventory
  };
}
```

## Game Server Setup (Colyseus)

### Colyseus Room Architecture
```typescript
// apps/server/src/rooms/TownRoom.ts
import { Room, Client } from '@colyseus/core';
import { WorldState } from './schemas/WorldState';

export class TownRoom extends Room<WorldState> {
  maxClients = 100;
  patchRate = 100; // 10 ticks/sec (1000ms / 10 = 100ms)

  onCreate(options: any) {
    this.setState(new WorldState());
    this.setSimulationInterval((deltaTime) => this.update(deltaTime));
    // Initialize NPC schedules
    // Start game clock
  }

  onJoin(client: Client, options: any) {
    // Add player to world state
    // Sync current NPC positions
  }

  onLeave(client: Client) {
    // Remove player from world state
  }

  update(deltaTime: number) {
    // Update game clock
    // Move NPCs along scheduled paths
    // Check for NPC-NPC proximity interactions
    // Broadcast state patches
  }
}
```

### State Schema (Colyseus)
```typescript
// apps/server/src/rooms/schemas/WorldState.ts
import { Schema, MapSchema, type } from '@colyseus/schema';

class Position extends Schema {
  @type('number') x: number = 0;
  @type('number') y: number = 0;
}

class PlayerState extends Schema {
  @type('string') id: string = '';
  @type('string') name: string = '';
  @type(Position) position = new Position();
  @type('string') animation: string = 'idle_down';
}

class NPCState extends Schema {
  @type('string') id: string = '';
  @type('string') name: string = '';
  @type(Position) position = new Position();
  @type('string') animation: string = 'idle_down';
  @type('string') currentAction: string = ''; // Icon above head
  @type('string') emotion: string = 'neutral'; // Emotion bubble
  @type('boolean') inDialogue: boolean = false;
}

class GameClockState extends Schema {
  @type('number') hour: number = 6;    // 0-23
  @type('number') minute: number = 0;  // 0-59
  @type('number') day: number = 1;
  @type('string') season: string = 'spring';
  @type('string') weather: string = 'sunny';
}

export class WorldState extends Schema {
  @type({ map: PlayerState }) players = new MapSchema<PlayerState>();
  @type({ map: NPCState }) npcs = new MapSchema<NPCState>();
  @type(GameClockState) clock = new GameClockState();
}
```

### Server Dependencies (apps/server)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@colyseus/core": "^0.15.0",
    "@colyseus/schema": "^2.0.0",

    "ioredis": "^5.3.0",
    "pg": "^8.11.0",
    "bullmq": "^5.0.0"
  }
}
```

### Game Clock Implementation
```
1 game hour = 1 real minute
1 game day = 24 real minutes
1 season = 7 game days = 168 real minutes (~2.8 hours)
1 year = 4 seasons = 28 game days = ~11.2 real hours

Server tick at 10/sec:
  - Each tick advances game clock by: 60 / (10 * 60) = 0.1 game-seconds per tick
  - Or equivalently: 100ms real time = 6 game-seconds
```

## AI Service Setup

### API Endpoints
| Endpoint | Method | Purpose | LLM Model | Avg Latency |
|----------|--------|---------|-----------|-------------|
| `/agent/dialogue` | POST | NPC responds to player | Sonnet | 1-2s |
| `/agent/plan` | POST | Generate daily plan | Haiku | 0.5-1s |
| `/agent/reflect` | POST | Generate daily reflections | Sonnet | 1-2s |
| `/agent/react` | POST | React to world event | Haiku | 0.3-0.5s |
| `/agent/conversation` | POST | NPC-to-NPC dialogue | Haiku | 0.5-1s |
| `/agent/importance` | POST | Score memory importance | Haiku | 0.2-0.3s |

### LLM Cost Optimization
```
Tiering System:
  FULL     - NPC is in dialogue with player      -> Full Sonnet calls
  NEARBY   - NPC is in player's viewport          -> Cached/Haiku responses
  BACKGROUND - NPC is off-screen                  -> Deterministic plan execution, no LLM

Caching Strategy:
  - Daily plans: generated once per game-day, cached in Redis
  - Greetings: common greetings cached per NPC (TTL: 1 game-day)
  - Importance scores: cached per event type pattern
  - Reflections: batched for all NPCs once per game-day

Target Budget: < $0.03 per player per hour
```

### AI Service Dependencies (apps/ai-service)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.30.0",
    "express": "^4.18.0",
    "ioredis": "^5.3.0",
    "pg": "^8.11.0",
    "bullmq": "^5.0.0",
    "pgvector": "^0.2.0"
  }
}
```

## Database Schema (PostgreSQL)

### Core Tables
```sql
-- Players
CREATE TABLE players (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  username VARCHAR(32) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  server_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
  reputation_level INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
  coins INTEGER DEFAULT 100,
  stars INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  homestead_data JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  inventory JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  last_login TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- NPC Agents
CREATE TABLE npc_agents (
  id VARCHAR(64) PRIMARY KEY,  -- e.g., 'marko_001'
  server_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
  seed_persona JSONB NOT NULL,
  current_plan JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  current_state JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  relationships JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Memory Stream
CREATE TABLE memories (
  id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  agent_id VARCHAR(64) REFERENCES npc_agents(id),
  server_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
  game_timestamp VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL,  -- 'Day 42, 14:30'
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  importance SMALLINT CHECK (importance BETWEEN 1 AND 10),
  memory_type VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,  -- 'observation', 'interaction', 'reflection'
  related_entities TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  embedding VECTOR(1536),  -- For semantic similarity search (pgvector)
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_memories_agent ON memories(agent_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_memories_importance ON memories(agent_id, importance DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_memories_embedding ON memories USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops);

-- Telemetry Events
CREATE TABLE telemetry_events (
  id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  event_name VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
  player_id UUID REFERENCES players(id),
  server_id VARCHAR(64),
  parameters JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_telemetry_event ON telemetry_events(event_name, created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_telemetry_player ON telemetry_events(player_id, created_at);
```

## Cross-Platform Considerations

### Performance Optimization
| Platform | Frame Rate Target | Memory Limit | Special Considerations |
|----------|------------------|--------------|----------------------|
| Desktop Browser (Chrome/Firefox/Safari) | 60 FPS | 512 MB | WebGL 2.0, keyboard+mouse input |
| Mobile Browser (Chrome/Safari) | 30 FPS | 256 MB | WebGL 1.0 fallback, touch input, battery life |

### Asset Optimization
```
Sprite Sheets:
  - Character sprites: 16x16 per frame, 4 directions, 4 frames each = 256x64 per character
  - Tilesets: 16x16 tiles, packed into 512x512 or 1024x1024 atlases
  - PNG format with indexed color (8-bit) for smallest size

Audio:
  - Music: OGG Vorbis, 128kbps, streamed (not preloaded)
  - SFX: OGG Vorbis, 64kbps, preloaded
  - Mobile: lower bitrate variants

Loading Strategy:
  - Boot scene loads core assets (player, UI, common tiles)
  - Zone assets loaded on-demand (lazy loading per zone)
  - CDN (CloudFlare) for all static assets
  - Service Worker for offline caching of loaded assets
```

### Mobile Adaptation
- Virtual joystick (nipplejs or custom) for movement
- Tap-to-interact replaces keyboard interaction
- Full-screen dialogue window on mobile (no side-by-side portrait)
- Larger touch targets for UI buttons (minimum 44x44 CSS pixels)
- Responsive HUD layout

## Development Environment Setup

### Prerequisites
```bash
# Node.js 22+ (LTS)
# npm 10+
# PostgreSQL 16+
# Redis 7+

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Generate Nx projects
npx nx g @nx/node:app server     # Colyseus game server
npx nx g @nx/node:app ai-service # NPC AI microservice
npx nx g @nx/js:lib shared       # Shared types library
```

### Development Commands
```bash
# Start all services for local development
npx nx dev game            # Next.js dev server (port 3000)
npx nx serve server        # Colyseus server (port 2567)
npx nx serve ai-service    # AI service (port 3001)

# Build for production
npx nx run-many -t build

# Run tests
npx nx run-many -t test
npx nx e2e game-e2e

# Lint and typecheck
npx nx run-many -t lint typecheck
```

### Environment Variables
```env
# apps/game/.env.local
NEXT_PUBLIC_COLYSEUS_URL=ws://localhost:2567
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3001

# apps/server/.env
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/nookstead
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
AI_SERVICE_URL=http://localhost:3001
COLYSEUS_PORT=2567

# apps/ai-service/.env
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/nookstead
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
AI_SERVICE_PORT=3001
```

## Quality Assurance Integration

### Testing Framework
```
Unit Tests:
  - Jest for all TypeScript code (already configured via @nx/jest)
  - Phaser scene tests with mocked game objects
  - Colyseus room tests with simulated clients
  - AI service tests with mocked Claude API responses

Integration Tests:
  - Client-server WebSocket communication
  - AI service dialogue flow end-to-end
  - Memory retrieval accuracy

E2E Tests:
  - Playwright (already configured via @nx/playwright)
  - Player movement and map rendering
  - NPC dialogue flow
  - Multiplayer join/leave

Performance Tests:
  - Lighthouse scores for initial page load
  - FPS measurement under load (multiple NPCs + players)
  - WebSocket throughput testing
  - LLM latency and cost tracking
```

### CI/CD Pipeline
```yaml
# Extends existing .github/workflows/ci.yml
# Add server and ai-service to the build matrix
npx nx run-many -t lint test build typecheck e2e

# Additional steps for prototype phase:
# - Docker build for server and ai-service
# - Deploy to staging environment
# - Run load tests against staging
```
